Small modular reactors | Darlington SMR – OPG
download_details.error: fetch error: HTTPError: 403 Client Error: Forbidden for url: https://www.opg.com/projects-services/projects/nuclear/smr/darlington-smr/

findings: Ontario Power Generation (OPG) selected the BWRX-300 SMR for use at the Darlington Nuclear Generating Station on December 1, 2021.
In October 2022, OPG applied for a construction license for the BWRX-300 reactor at Darlington, with plans to start operations in 2028.
On July 7, 2023, OPG ordered three additional BWRX-300 SMRs for construction at the Darlington New Nuclear Project, bringing the total to four units.

A final investment decision in May 2025 to proceed with building the first BWRX-300 at Darlington was based on a forecast cost of Canadian $7.7 billion (US$5.6 billion), with an estimated Canadian $13.2 billion (US$9.6 billion) for three further units.

The first BWRX-300 is under construction at Ontario Power Generation's Darlington site in Canada, with completion expected by the end of the decade, which will make it the first small modular reactor in the Western World.
